    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 340,
            "text": "def _append_skip_pair(path: Path, slug: str, year: int, seen: set[tuple[str, int]]) -\u003e None:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 341,
            "text": "    key = (str(slug).strip(), int(year))"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 342,
            "text": "    if not key[0] or key in seen:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 343,
            "text": "        return"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 344,
            "text": "    seen.add(key)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 345,
            "text": "    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 346,
            "text": "    new_file = not path.is_file() or path.stat().st_size == 0"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 347,
            "text": "    with open(path, \"a\", newline=\"\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 348,
            "text": "        w = csv.writer(f)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 349,
            "text": "        if new_file:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 350,
            "text": "            w.writerow([\"school_slug\", \"sr_year\"])"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 351,
            "text": "        w.writerow([key[0], key[1]])"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 352
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 353
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 354,
            "text": "def _read_out_raw_safe(path: Path) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 355,
            "text": "    try:"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 358
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "column": 5,
            "line": 336
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./sports_pipeline/scrape_bpm.py",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.1667892038822174
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "\"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "Rebuild the 530 **player–season–team** panel from data on disk (no legacy 530 notebook)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "**Flow (gameplan-aligned):**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "1. Aggregate &#96;&#96;mbb_df_player_box.csv&#96;&#96; → one row per &#96;&#96;(athlete_id, season, team_id)&#96;&#96; with"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "   &#96;&#96;minutes&#96;&#96;, &#96;&#96;points&#96;&#96;, &#96;&#96;ppm&#96;&#96;, &#96;&#96;games&#96;&#96;, display names."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "2. Attach **ever-draft** &#96;&#96;Y_draft&#96;&#96; from &#96;&#96;athlete_id_draft_lookup.csv&#96;&#96;."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "3. Left-merge **SR advanced** columns from &#96;&#96;DO_NOT_ERASE/bpm_player_season_matched.csv&#96;&#96; when"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "   that file exists (same keys as legacy Cell 7)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "Downstream, &#96;&#96;panel_build.apply_perf_metric_for_analysis&#96;&#96; sets &#96;&#96;perf&#96;&#96; and recomputes"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;poolq_loo&#96;&#96; / &#96;&#96;poolq_sq&#96;&#96; for ventiles + LPM (PPM, minutes, BPM, OBPM, DBPM)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "See also &#96;&#96;bpm_merge&#96;&#96; for crosswalk / scrape jobs / raw→matched refresh."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "\"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "from __future__ import annotations"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "from pathlib import Path"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "from typing import Any"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "import numpy as np"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "import pandas as pd"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "from sports_pipeline import paths"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "def merge_sr_matched_into_panel(df: pd.DataFrame, matched_path: Any = None) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "    Left-merge &#96;&#96;bpm_player_season_matched.csv&#96;&#96; on &#96;&#96;(athlete_id, season, team_id)&#96;&#96;."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33,
            "text": "    Drops overlapping stat columns from &#96;&#96;df&#96;&#96; before merge so refreshed SR values win."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "    p = Path(paths.bpm_matched_csv() if matched_path is None else matched_path)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "    if not p.is_file():"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37,
            "text": "        return df"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "    mb = pd.read_csv(p, low_memory=False)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39,
            "text": "    key = [\"athlete_id\", \"season\", \"team_id\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "    for c in key:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41,
            "text": "        if c not in df.columns or c not in mb.columns:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "            return df"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43,
            "text": "    for col in key:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "text": "        df = df.copy()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45,
            "text": "        mb = mb.copy()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46,
            "text": "        df[col] = pd.to_numeric(df[col], errors=\"coerce\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47,
            "text": "        mb[col] = pd.to_numeric(mb[col], errors=\"coerce\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 48,
            "text": "    mb = mb.drop_duplicates(subset=key)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 49,
            "text": "    extra = [c for c in mb.columns if c not in key and c in df.columns]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 50,
            "text": "    out = df.drop(columns=extra, errors=\"ignore\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 51,
            "text": "    stat_cols = [c for c in mb.columns if c not in key]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 52,
            "text": "    out = out.merge(mb[key + stat_cols], on=key, how=\"left\")"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 53,
            "text": "    return out"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 54
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 55
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 56,
            "text": "def build_from_box(cfg: Any) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 57,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 119
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./sports_pipeline/panel_rebuild.py",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.16573479771614075
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "def summarize_data_integrity(panel: pd.DataFrame, cfg: Any) -\u003e str:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "    BOX (game-level CSV), draft register, draft→athlete match file, and the same"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41,
            "text": "    &#96;filter_panel&#96; subset used for ventile plots. Large BOX read (3 cols only)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43,
            "text": "    buf = StringIO()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "text": "    w = buf.write"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46,
            "text": "    use = filter_panel(panel, cfg)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47,
            "text": "    s_lo, s_hi = _season_window_from_use(use, cfg)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 48,
            "text": "    dy_lo, dy_hi = _draft_year_window(s_lo, s_hi, cfg)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 49
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 50,
            "text": "    w(\"=== Data integrity (aligned with ventile-plot sample) ===\\n\\n\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 51
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 52,
            "text": "    w(\"**Analysis window (collegiate seasons in plot sample &#96;use&#96;)**\\n\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 53,
            "text": "    w(f\"- Seasons: {s_lo}–{s_hi} (override with CFG.analysis_season_min / max if set)\\n\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 54,
            "text": "    w("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 55,
            "text": "        f\"- After &#96;dropna(poolq_loo, Y_draft)&#96;\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 56,
            "text": "        + ("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 57,
            "text": "            f\" and minutes ≥ {getattr(cfg, 'min_minutes', 0)}\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 58,
            "text": "            if float(getattr(cfg, \"min_minutes\", 0)) \u003e 0"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 59,
            "text": "            else \"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 60,
            "text": "        )"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 61,
            "text": "        + \":\\n\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 62,
            "text": "    )"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 63,
            "text": "    w(f\"  - Player–season rows: {len(use):,}\\n\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 64,
            "text": "    w(f\"  - Unique athletes: {use['athlete_id'].nunique():,}\\n\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 65,
            "text": "    w(f\"  - Unique teams (team_id): {use['team_id'].nunique():,}\\n\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 66,
            "text": "    w(f\"  - Share of rows with Y_draft=1: {use['Y_draft'].mean():.2%}\\n\\n\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 67
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 68,
            "text": "    w(\"**BOX (&#96;mbb_df_player_box.csv&#96;, game-level; athlete_id × season × team_id)**\\n\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 69,
            "text": "    p_box = paths.player_box_csv()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 70,
            "text": "    if not p_box.is_file():"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 71,
            "text": "        w(f\"- FILE MISSING: {p_box}\\n\\n\")"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 168
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "column": 29,
            "line": 34
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./sports_pipeline/data_integrity.py",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.1641869992017746
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 433,
            "text": "def run_batch("
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 434,
            "text": "    scrape_cfg: ScrapeBpmConfig | None = None,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 435,
            "text": "    *,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 436,
            "text": "    pipeline_cfg: Any | None = None,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 437,
            "text": ") -\u003e dict[str, Any]:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 438,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 439,
            "text": "    Fetch team-season advanced tables and append to &#96;&#96;bpm_player_season_raw.csv&#96;&#96;."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 440
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 441,
            "text": "    If &#96;&#96;pipeline_cfg&#96;&#96; is passed, merges &#96;&#96;sr_scrape_contact&#96;&#96; and &#96;&#96;sr_scrape_max_fetches&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 442,
            "text": "    when the scrape config leaves defaults."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 443,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 444,
            "text": "    requests_mod, BeautifulSoup = _require_scrape_deps()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 445,
            "text": "    cfg = scrape_cfg or ScrapeBpmConfig()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 446,
            "text": "    if pipeline_cfg is not None:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 447,
            "text": "        contact = getattr(pipeline_cfg, \"sr_scrape_contact\", None)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 448,
            "text": "        if contact and cfg.user_agent is None:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 449,
            "text": "            cfg.default_user_agent_contact = str(contact)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 450,
            "text": "        mx = getattr(pipeline_cfg, \"sr_scrape_max_fetches\", None)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 451,
            "text": "        if mx is not None and cfg.max_fetches_per_run is None:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 452,
            "text": "            cfg.max_fetches_per_run = int(mx)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 453
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 454,
            "text": "    headers = _headers_for_config(cfg)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 455,
            "text": "    pairs = _load_job_pairs(cfg)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 456,
            "text": "    out_raw = Path(cfg.out_raw_csv)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 457,
            "text": "    skip_path = Path(cfg.skip_pairs_csv)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 458,
            "text": "    alias_path = Path(cfg.aliases_csv)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 459
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 460,
            "text": "    if not cfg.resume_skip_scraped and out_raw.is_file():"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 461,
            "text": "        out_raw.unlink()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 462,
            "text": "        print(\"resume_skip_scraped False — cleared\", out_raw.name)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 463
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 464,
            "text": "    _ensure_alias_csv(alias_path)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 465,
            "text": "    alias_seen = _alias_panel_slugs_in_csv(alias_path)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 466,
            "text": "    refresh_sr_slug_alias_cache()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 467,
            "text": "    skip_seen = _read_skip_pairs(skip_path)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 468
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 469,
            "text": "    have: set[tuple[str, int]] = set()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 470,
            "text": "    if cfg.resume_skip_scraped and out_raw.is_file() and out_raw.stat().st_size \u003e 0:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 471,
            "text": "        ex = _read_out_raw_safe(out_raw)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 472,
            "text": "        if \"school_slug\" in ex.columns and \"sr_year\" in ex.columns:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 473,
            "text": "            sy = pd.to_numeric(ex[\"sr_year\"], errors=\"coerce\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 474,
            "text": "            for sl, y in zip(ex[\"school_slug\"].astype(str).str.strip(), sy):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 475,
            "text": "                if pd.notna(y):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 476,
            "text": "                    have.add((sl, int(y)))"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 477,
            "text": "        print(f\"Resume: {len(have)} team-seasons already in {out_raw.name}\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 478
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 479,
            "text": "    def _row_key(r: pd.Series) -\u003e tuple[str, int]:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 480,
            "text": "        return (str(r[\"school_slug\"]).strip(), int(r[\"sr_year\"]))"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 481
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 482,
            "text": "    pending_mask = pairs.apply("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 483,
            "text": "        lambda r:"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 605
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "column": 5,
            "line": 429
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./sports_pipeline/scrape_bpm.py",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.16311359405517578
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 121,
            "text": "def run_match("
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 122,
            "text": "    cfg: Any | None = None,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 123,
            "text": "    *,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 124,
            "text": "    panel: pd.DataFrame | None = None,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 125,
            "text": "    raw_path: Path | None = None,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 126,
            "text": "    crosswalk: pd.DataFrame | None = None,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 127,
            "text": ") -\u003e dict[str, Any]:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 170
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 171,
            "text": "    else:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 172,
            "text": "        cw = crosswalk"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 173
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 174,
            "text": "    if \"school_slug\" in panel_df.columns:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 175,
            "text": "        panel_df = panel_df.drop(columns=[\"school_slug\"])"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 176,
            "text": "    panel_df = panel_df.merge(cw[[\"team_id\", \"school_slug\"]], on=\"team_id\", how=\"left\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 177,
            "text": "    panel_df[\"player_key\"] = panel_df[\"athlete_display_name\"].map(normalize_player_name)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 178,
            "text": "    bpm = bpm.copy()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 179,
            "text": "    bpm[\"player_key\"] = bpm[\"Player\"].map(normalize_player_name)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 180,
            "text": "    bpm[\"sr_year\"] = bpm[\"sr_year\"].astype(int)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 181,
            "text": "    panel_df[\"season\"] = panel_df[\"season\"].astype(int)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 182
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 183,
            "text": "    bpm_sort = bpm.sort_values("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 184,
            "text": "        [\"school_slug\", \"sr_year\", \"player_key\", \"MP\"],"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 185,
            "text": "        ascending=[True, True, True, False],"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 186,
            "text": "    )"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 187,
            "text": "    bpm_dedup = bpm_sort.drop_duplicates("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 188,
            "text": "        subset=[\"school_slug\", \"sr_year\", \"player_key\"], keep=\"first\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 189,
            "text": "    )"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 190
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 191,
            "text": "    stat_cols = ["
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 192,
            "text": "        c"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 193,
            "text": "        for c in [\"PER\", \"TS%\", \"WS\", \"WS/40\", \"OBPM\", \"DBPM\", \"BPM\", \"MP\", \"G\", \"GS\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 194,
            "text": "        if c in bpm_dedup.columns"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 195,
            "text": "    ]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 196,
            "text": "    rename_map = {\"MP\": \"mp_sr\", \"TS%\": \"ts_pct_sr\"}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 197,
            "text": "    bpm_sub = bpm_dedup[[\"school_slug\", \"sr_year\", \"player_key\"] + stat_cols].rename("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 198,
            "text": "        columns=rename_map"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 199,
            "text": "    )"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 200
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 201,
            "text": "    # &#96;&#96;build_from_box&#96;&#96; left-merges the previous &#96;&#96;bpm_player_season_matched.csv&#96;&#96; onto the"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 202,
            "text": "    # panel, so &#96;&#96;panel_df&#96;&#96; may already contain BPM/OBPM/... . A second merge with &#96;&#96;bpm_sub&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 203,
            "text": "    # collides on those names: pandas keeps the left column and renames the right to &#96;&#96;*_drop&#96;&#96;,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 204,
            "text": "    # and we drop &#96;&#96;*_drop&#96;&#96; below — which discards fresh SR stats and leaves only stale seasons"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 205,
            "text": "    # (e.g. 2011–2015) on the left. Drop overlapping SR columns before merging."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 206,
            "text": "    _keep = {\"school_slug\", \"player_key\"}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 207,
            "text": "    _overlap = [c for c in bpm_sub.columns if c in panel_df.columns and c not in _keep]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 208,
            "text": "    panel_df = panel_df.drop(columns=_overlap, errors=\"ignore\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 209
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 210,
            "text": "    merged = panel_df.merge("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 211,
            "text": "        bpm_sub,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 212,
            "text": "        left_on=[\"school_slug\", \"season\", \"player_key\"],"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 213,
            "text": "        right_on=[\"school_slug\", \"sr_year\", \"player_key\"],"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 214,
            "text": "        how=\"left\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 215,
            "text": "        suffixes=(\"\", \"_drop\"),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 216,
            "text": "    )"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 217,
            "text": "    merged = merged.drop(columns=[c for c in merged.columns if c.endswith(\"_drop\")], errors=\"ignore\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 218,
            "text": "    merged = merged.drop(columns=[\"sr_year\"], errors=\"ignore\")"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 266
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 120
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./sports_pipeline/bpm_merge.py",
        "signatures": {
          "ranges": [
            {
              "endPosition": {
                "column": 5,
                "line": 128
              },
              "startPosition": {
                "column": 1,
                "line": 121
              }
            }
          ]
        }
      },
      "score": 0.15842460095882416
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 50,
            "text": "def ensure_crosswalk(cfg: Any | None = None) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 51,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 52,
            "text": "    Load or create &#96;&#96;sr_school_slug_crosswalk.csv&#96;&#96; from &#96;&#96;mbb_df_team_box.csv&#96;&#96;."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 53
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 54,
            "text": "    Preserves user-edited &#96;&#96;school_slug&#96;&#96; values for known &#96;&#96;team_id&#96;&#96;s; fills new teams"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 55,
            "text": "    with &#96;&#96;suggest_school_slug&#96;&#96;."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 56,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 57,
            "text": "    _ = cfg"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 58,
            "text": "    team_box = paths.team_box_csv()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 59,
            "text": "    cross_path = paths.sr_crosswalk_csv()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 60,
            "text": "    cross_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 61
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 62,
            "text": "    usecols = [\"team_id\", \"team_short_display_name\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 63,
            "text": "    tb = pd.read_csv(team_box, usecols=usecols, low_memory=False)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 64,
            "text": "    tb = tb.dropna(subset=[\"team_id\", \"team_short_display_name\"]).drop_duplicates("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 65,
            "text": "        subset=[\"team_id\", \"team_short_display_name\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 66,
            "text": "    )"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 67
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 68,
            "text": "    if cross_path.is_file():"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 69,
            "text": "        existing = pd.read_csv(cross_path, low_memory=False)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 70,
            "text": "        if \"school_slug\" not in existing.columns:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 71,
            "text": "            raise ValueError(f\"{cross_path} must contain column school_slug\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 72,
            "text": "        base = tb.merge("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 73,
            "text": "            existing[[\"team_id\", \"school_slug\"]].drop_duplicates(\"team_id\"),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 74,
            "text": "            on=\"team_id\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 75,
            "text": "            how=\"left\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 76,
            "text": "        )"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 77,
            "text": "        miss = base[\"school_slug\"].isna()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 78,
            "text": "        if miss.any():"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 79,
            "text": "            base.loc[miss, \"school_slug\"] = base.loc[miss, \"team_short_display_name\"].map("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 80,
            "text": "                suggest_school_slug"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 81,
            "text": "            )"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 82,
            "text": "        out = base[[\"team_id\", \"team_short_display_name\", \"school_slug\"]].copy()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 83,
            "text": "    else:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 84,
            "text": "        out = tb.copy()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 85,
            "text": "        out[\"school_slug\"] = out[\"team_short_display_name\"].map(suggest_school_slug)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 86
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 87,
            "text": "    out = out.drop_duplicates(subset=[\"team_id\"], keep=\"first\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 88,
            "text": "    out.to_csv(cross_path, index=False)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 89,
            "text": "    print(f\"Crosswalk: {len(out)} rows → {cross_path}\")"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 90,
            "text": "    return out"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 91
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 92
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 93,

      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 381,
            "text": "def run_ventile_eda(df: pd.DataFrame, cfg: Any) -\u003e tuple[pd.DataFrame, Path]:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 382,
            "text": "    \"\"\"Filter → binned table CSV → ventile PNG (with optional quadratic overlay).\"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 383,
            "text": "    use = filter_panel(df, cfg)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 384,
            "text": "    bin_tab = ventile_table(use, cfg)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 385,
            "text": "    out_dir = Path(getattr(cfg, \"exports_dir\", paths.mbb_dir() / \"exports_inverted_u_v0\"))"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 386,
            "text": "    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 387,
            "text": "    stamp = date.today().isoformat()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 388,
            "text": "    plot_slug = export_plot_slug(cfg)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 389,
            "text": "    csv_path = out_dir / f\"binned_draft_rate_ventiles_{plot_slug}_{stamp}.csv\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 390,
            "text": "    bin_tab.to_csv(csv_path, index=False)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 391
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 392,
            "text": "    prov_lines = ventile_provenance_lines(use, cfg, bin_tab) if len(use) else []"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 393,
            "text": "    prov_path = out_dir / f\"ventile_eda_provenance_{plot_slug}_{stamp}.txt\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 394,
            "text": "    prov_path.write_text(\"\\n\".join(prov_lines) + \"\\n\", encoding=\"utf-8\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 395,
            "text": "    print(\"--- Ventile EDA — sample / datasets / per-ventile n ---\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 396,
            "text": "    print(\"\\n\".join(prov_lines))"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 397,
            "text": "    print(f\"(Also wrote {prov_path.name})\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 398,
            "text": "    print()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 399
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 400,
            "text": "    png_path = ventile_plot("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 401,
            "text": "        bin_tab, cfg, len(use), use=use, provenance_lines=prov_lines if prov_lines else None"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 402,
            "text": "    )"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 403,
            "text": "    return bin_tab, png_path"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 404
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 405
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 406,
            "text": "def run_dirty_lpm(df: pd.DataFrame, cfg: Any) -\u003e Any | pd.Series:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 407,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          }
        ],

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 314,
            "text": "def _append_404_panel_slug_now(path: Path, slug: str, seen: set[str]) -\u003e None:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 315,
            "text": "    s = str(slug).strip()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 316,
            "text": "    if not s or s in seen:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 317,
            "text": "        return"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 318,
            "text": "    seen.add(s)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 319,
            "text": "    with open(path, \"a\", newline=\"\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 320,
            "text": "        w = csv.writer(f)"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 321,
            "text": "        w.writerow([s, \"\"])"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 322
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 323
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 324,
            "text": "def _read_skip_pairs(path: Path) -\u003e set[tuple[str, int]]:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 325,
            "text": "    if not path.is_file() or path.stat().st_size == 0:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 326,
            "text": "        return set()"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 336
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "column": 56,
            "line": 310
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./sports_pipeline/scrape_bpm.py",
        "signatures": {}

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 54,
            "text": "def run_conductor_from_panel(cfg: Any, panel: pd.DataFrame) -\u003e dict[str, Any]:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 55,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 56,
            "text": "    Run perf → integrity → coverage → ventiles → export on &#96;&#96;panel&#96;&#96; without reloading it."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 57
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 58,
            "text": "    &#96;&#96;panel&#96;&#96; must be the pre-perf frame (same as &#96;&#96;prepare_panel&#96;&#96; returns). Pass"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 59,
            "text": "    &#96;&#96;panel.copy(deep=True)&#96;&#96; per metric when looping so each run starts from a clean base."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 60,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 61,
            "text": "    out: dict[str, Any] = {\"stage\": \"530_conductor\"}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 62,
            "text": "    out[\"panel_shape_before_perf\"] = tuple(panel.shape)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 63
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 64,
            "text": "    panel_work = panel_build.apply_perf_metric_for_analysis("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 65,
            "text": "        panel,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 66,
            "text": "        getattr(cfg, \"perf_metric\", \"minutes\"),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 67,
            "text": "        poolq_winsor_quantiles=getattr(cfg, \"poolq_winsor_quantiles\", None),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 68,
            "text": "        zscore_perf_within_season=bool(getattr(cfg, \"perf_zscore_within_season\", False)),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 69,
            "text": "    )"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 70,
            "text": "    out[\"PANEL\"] = panel_work"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 71
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 72,
            "text": "    if bool(getattr(cfg, \"run_data_integrity_report\", True)):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 73,
            "text": "        print(\"--- Data integrity ---\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 74,
            "text": "        data_integrity.print_data_integrity(panel_work, cfg)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 75,
            "text": "        print()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 76
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 77,
            "text": "    if bool(getattr(cfg, \"run_yearly_coverage_chart\", True)):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 78,
            "text": "        tab, png = dataset_coverage.export_yearly_coverage_chart(cfg, show=True)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 79,
            "text": "        out[\"coverage_table\"] = tab"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 80,
            "text": "        out[\"coverage_png\"] = png"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 81
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 82,
            "text": "    if bool(getattr(cfg, \"run_ventile_eda\", True)):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 83,
            "text": "        bin_tab, png = panel_build.run_ventile_eda(panel_work, cfg)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 84,
            "text": "        out[\"ventile_table\"] = bin_tab"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 85,
            "text": "        out[\"ventile_png\"] = png"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 86
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 87,
            "text": "    if bool(getattr(cfg, \"run_dirty_lpm\", True)):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 88,
            "text": "        lpm = panel_build.run_dirty_lpm(panel_work, cfg)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 89,
            "text": "        out[\"lpm\"] = lpm"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 90,
            "text": "        if hasattr(lpm, \"summary\"):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 91,
            "text": "            print(\"--- LPM (statsmodels) ---\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 92,
            "text": "            print(lpm.summary())"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 93,
            "text": "        else:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 94,
            "text": "            print(\"--- LPM coefficients (numpy OLS) ---\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 95,
            "text": "            print(lpm)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 96
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 97,
            "text": "    if bool(getattr(cfg, \"export_panel_after_run\", False)):"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 107
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "column": 30,
            "line": 50
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./sports_pipeline/conductor.py",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.15140220522880554

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 111,
            "text": "def run_conductor(cfg: Any) -\u003e dict[str, Any]:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 112,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 113,
            "text": "    Run the 530 pipeline according to boolean flags on &#96;&#96;cfg&#96;&#96;."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 114
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 115,
            "text": "    Returns a dict including &#96;&#96;\"PANEL\"&#96;&#96; (post perf/LOO) and optional stage outputs."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 116,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 117,
            "text": "    if bool(getattr(cfg, \"run_stage3_status\", False)):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 118,
            "text": "        print(\"--- Stage 3 status (stubs / optional scrape) ---\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 119,
            "text": "        print(ingest_box.run(cfg))"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 120,
            "text": "        print(draft_match.run(cfg))"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 121,
            "text": "        print(sr_bpm.run(cfg))"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 122,
            "text": "        print()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 123
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 124,
            "text": "    panel = _prepare_panel(cfg)"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 125,
            "text": "    return run_conductor_from_panel(cfg, panel)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 126
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 127
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 128,
            "text": "def panel_has_metric_column(panel: pd.DataFrame, metric: str) -\u003e bool:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 129,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 130

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 388,
            "text": "def _load_job_pairs(cfg: ScrapeBpmConfig) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 389,
            "text": "    p = cfg.jobs_csv"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 390,
            "text": "    if p.is_file() and not cfg.force_demo_slugs:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 391,
            "text": "        jobs = pd.read_csv(p, low_memory=False)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 392,
            "text": "        for c in (\"school_slug\", \"sr_year\"):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 393,
            "text": "            if c not in jobs.columns:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 394,
            "text": "                raise ValueError(f\"{p} must include columns school_slug, sr_year; got {list(jobs.columns)}\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 395,
            "text": "        jobs = jobs.dropna(subset=[\"school_slug\", \"sr_year\"]).copy()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 396,
            "text": "        jobs[\"sr_year\"] = pd.to_numeric(jobs[\"sr_year\"], errors=\"coerce\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 397,
            "text": "        jobs = jobs.dropna(subset=[\"sr_year\"])"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 398,
            "text": "        jobs[\"school_slug\"] = jobs[\"school_slug\"].astype(str).str.strip()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 399,
            "text": "        jobs = jobs[jobs[\"school_slug\"].str.len() \u003e 0]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 400,
            "text": "        return ("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 401,
            "text": "            jobs[[\"school_slug\", \"sr_year\"]]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 402,
            "text": "            .drop_duplicates()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 403,
            "text": "            .sort_values([\"sr_year\", \"school_slug\"], kind=\"mergesort\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 404,
            "text": "            .reset_index(drop=True)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 405,
            "text": "        )"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 406,
            "text": "    demo = pd.DataFrame("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 407,
            "text": "        {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 408,
            "text": "            \"school_slug\": ["
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 409,
            "text": "                \"duke\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 410,
            "text": "                \"north-carolina\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 411,
            "text": "                \"gonzaga\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 412,
            "text": "                \"virginia\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 413,
            "text": "                \"connecticut\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 414,
            "text": "            ],"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 415,
            "text": "            \"sr_year\": 2024,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 416,
            "text": "        }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 417,
            "text": "    )"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 418,
            "text": "    print(\"No jobs CSV (or force_demo_slugs) — demo schools × single season.\")"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 419,
            "text": "    return demo"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 420
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 421
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 422,
            "text": "def _headers_for_config(cfg: ScrapeBpmConfig) -\u003e dict[str, str]:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 423,
            "text": "    ua = cfg.user_agent"
          }
        ],
        "range": {

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 56,
            "text": "def build_from_box(cfg: Any) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 110
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 111,
            "text": "    if \"athlete_id\" not in lu.columns:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 112,
            "text": "        raise ValueError(f\"Draft lookup must have athlete_id: {lu_path}\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 113,
            "text": "    drafted = set(pd.to_numeric(lu[\"athlete_id\"], errors=\"coerce\").dropna().astype(int))"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 114,
            "text": "    agg[\"athlete_id\"] = pd.to_numeric(agg[\"athlete_id\"], errors=\"coerce\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 115,
            "text": "    agg = agg.dropna(subset=[\"athlete_id\"])"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 116,
            "text": "    agg[\"athlete_id\"] = agg[\"athlete_id\"].astype(int)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 117,
            "text": "    agg[\"Y_draft\"] = agg[\"athlete_id\"].isin(drafted).astype(int)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 118
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 119,
            "text": "    agg = merge_sr_matched_into_panel(agg)"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 120,
            "text": "    return agg"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 14,
            "line": 119
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 55
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./sports_pipeline/panel_rebuild.py",
        "signatures": {
          "ranges": [
            {

{
  "result": "{\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"### Performance metrics (&#96;perf_metric&#96;) and z-scores\\n\\n**ESPN box (no SR merge)**\\n\\n- **&#96;minutes&#96;** — Player–season–team total minutes from game-level box. Leave-one-out (LOO) pool quality = mean teammate **minutes** excluding self.\\n- **&#96;ppm&#96;** — Points per minute from box. LOO = mean teammate **PPM** excluding self.\\n\\n**Sports Reference** (needs &#96;DO_NOT_ERASE/bpm_player_season_matched.csv&#96; on the panel)\\n\\n- **&#96;bpm&#96;** — Box Plus/Minus; SR all-in-one advanced impact statistic.\\n- **&#96;obpm&#96;** / **&#96;opm&#96;** — Offensive BPM (same matched row as BPM).\\n- **&#96;dbpm&#96;** / **&#96;dpm&#96;** — Defensive BPM.\\n- **&#96;per&#96;** — Player efficiency rating; composite efficiency index.\\n- **&#96;ws40&#96;** — Win shares **per 40 minutes** (rate).\\n- **&#96;ws&#96;** — Win **shares** (season total at the matched row).\\n- **&#96;tspct&#96;** or **&#96;ts_pct&#96;** — True shooting **percentage** from SR (panel column &#96;ts_pct_sr&#96;).\\n\\n**&#96;perf_zscore_within_season&#96;** — If &#96;True&#96;, &#96;perf&#96; is replaced by its **z-score within each collegiate season** before LOO. Then &#96;poolq_loo&#96; is in **within-season SD units** (useful for comparing metrics or multi-metric plots later).\\n\\n**Export filenames** — Figures and CSVs under &#96;CFG.exports_dir&#96; include a slug from &#96;perf_metric&#96; plus &#96;_zwithinseason&#96; when z-scoring is on, e.g. &#96;inverted_u_ventiles_dbpm_2026-04-01.png&#96; vs &#96;dataset_coverage_dashboard_obpm_zwithinseason_2026-04-01.png&#96;, so each PowerPoint slide can match a distinct file.\"}],\"structuredContent\":{\"result\":\"### Performance metrics (&#96;perf_metric&#96;) and z-scores\\n\\n**ESPN box (no SR merge)**\\n\\n- **&#96;minutes&#96;** — Player–season–team total minutes from game-level box. Leave-one-out (LOO) pool quality = mean teammate **minutes** excluding self.\\n- **&#96;ppm&#96;** — Points per minute from box. LOO = mean teammate **PPM** excluding self.\\n\\n**Sports Reference** (needs &#96;DO_NOT_ERASE/bpm_player_season_matched.csv&#96; on the panel)\\n\\n- **&#96;bpm&#96;** — Box Plus/Minus; SR all-in-one advanced impact statistic.\\n- **&#96;obpm&#96;** / **&#96;opm&#96;** — Offensive BPM (same matched row as BPM).\\n- **&#96;dbpm&#96;** / **&#96;dpm&#96;** — Defensive BPM.\\n- **&#96;per&#96;** — Player efficiency rating; composite efficiency index.\\n- **&#96;ws40&#96;** — Win shares **per 40 minutes** (rate).\\n- **&#96;ws&#96;** — Win **shares** (season total at the matched row).\\n- **&#96;tspct&#96;** or **&#96;ts_pct&#96;** — True shooting **percentage** from SR (panel column &#96;ts_pct_sr&#96;).\\n\\n**&#96;perf_zscore_within_season&#96;** — If &#96;True&#96;, &#96;perf&#96; is replaced by its **z-score within each collegiate season** before LOO. Then &#96;poolq_loo&#96; is in **within-season SD units** (useful for comparing metrics or multi-metric plots later).\\n\\n**Export filenames** — Figures and CSVs under &#96;CFG.exports_dir&#96; include a slug from &#96;perf_metric&#96; plus &#96;_zwithinseason&#96; when z-scoring is on, e.g. &#96;inverted_u_ventiles_dbpm_2026-04-01.png&#96; vs &#96;dataset_coverage_dashboard_obpm_zwithinseason_2026-04-01.png&#96;, so each PowerPoint slide can match a distinct file.\"}}"

Source QA file: &#96;datasets/mbb/draft_unmapped_colleges.csv&#96; (from your draft-match export).\\n- **Sports Reference** — Same toggle prints ESPN **teams** (with &#96;team_id&#96;, display name, &#96;school_slug&#96;) that still have player-season rows without a BPM merge hit after the last &#96;run_match&#96;. _**Agent (default)**_

Here is what was added.

### Behavior

- **`CFG.print_unmatched_school_lists`** (default **`False`** in code; your notebook is set to **`True`**) triggers a printout **right after `prepare_panel`** (so it runs once per run, including the multi-metric path).
- **`CFG.unmatched_school_report_max_lines`** — use **`None`** to print every line in both sections; set an **int** to cap each section (full lists stay on disk).

### Draft (register → ESPN school map)

- Reads **`datasets/mbb/draft_unmapped_colleges.csv`** via new `paths.draft_unmapped_colleges_csv()`.
- Lists **college** strings that never mapped, with **`n_draft_picks`** / **`years`** when those columns exist.
- If the file is missing, the report explains that it comes from your draft-match QA export.

### Sports Reference (BPM merge)

- Aggregates **`datasets/mbb/bpm_panel_rows_unmatched.csv`** (from the last **`bpm_merge.run_match`**) by **`team_id`**, **`team_short_display_name`**, and **`school_slug`**, sorted by **`n_unmatched_rows`** (largest gaps first).
- Short note on usual causes: slug/crosswalk, missing scrape, name normalization.

### Code touchpoints

- `sports_pipeline/paths.py` — `draft_unmapped_colleges_csv()`
- `sports_pipeline/data_integrity.py` — `summarize_unmatched_schools_draft`, `summarize_unmatched_schools_sr`, `print_unmatched_school_reports`
- `sports_pipeline/config.py` — the two new fields
- `sports_pipeline/conductor.py` — `prepare_panel` calls the printer when the flag is set; **`run_conductor`** now uses **`prepare_panel`** (not `_prepare_panel` alone) so single-shot runs get the same behavior
- **`530_sports_pipeline.ipynb`** — CELL 2 comments + values; glossary cell documents sources and your use case (small colleges, HBCUs, imputation ideas)

A quick smoke test on your tree already surfaces names like **Jarvis Chr**, **Howard Payne**, **Bethesda**, **Reinhardt** in the SR section—aligned with the pattern you described.

If the console is too heavy with **`unmatched_school_report_max_lines=None`**, set it to e.g. **`400`** in CELL 2 and rely on the CSVs for the full lists.

---

When RUN_ALL_PERF_METRICS=True and run_yearly_coverage_chart=True, coverage\\n# runs once after prepare_panel; the sweep turns coverage off so you do not get duplicate PNGs/CSVs.\\n#\\nfrom dataclasses import replace\\n\\nRUN_ALL_PERF_METRICS = True\\n\\nif RUN_ALL_PERF_METRICS:\\n    if CFG.run_stage3_status:\\n        print(\\\"--- Stage 3 status (stubs / optional scrape) ---\\\")\\n        print(ingest_box.run(CFG))\\n        print(draft_match.run(CFG))\\n        print(sr_bpm.run(CFG))\\n        print()\\n    panel_base = conductor.prepare_panel(CFG)\\n    if CFG.run_yearly_coverage_chart:\\n        print(\\\"--- Yearly coverage exports (once; same for every perf_metric) ---\\\")\\n        dataset_coverage.export_yearly_coverage_chart(CFG, show=True)\\n    metrics = perf_metric.perf_options_df()[\\\"metric\\\"].tolist()\\n    OUT_BY_METRIC = {}\\n    skipped = []\\n    for m in metrics:\\n        if not conductor.panel_has_metric_column(panel_base, m):\\n            skipped.append(m)\\n            continue\\n        print(f\\\"\\\\n========== perf_metric={m!r} ==========\\\\n\\\")\\n        cfg_m = replace(CFG, perf_metric=m, run_yearly_coverage_chart=False)\\n        OUT_BY_METRIC[m] = conductor.run_conductor_from_panel(cfg_m, panel_base.copy(deep=True))\\n    if skipped:\\n        print(f\\\"--- Skipped (missing panel column): {skipped} ---\\\")\\n    OUT = OUT_BY_METRIC\\n    if OUT_BY_METRIC:\\n        PANEL = (\\n            OUT_BY_METRIC[CFG.perf_metric][\\\"PANEL\\\"]\\n            if CFG.perf_metric in OUT_BY_METRIC\\n            else list(OUT_BY_METRIC.values())[-1][\\\"PANEL\\\"]\\n        )\\n    else:\\n        PANEL = None\\nelse:\\n    OUT = conductor.run_conductor(CFG)\\n    PANEL = OUT[\\\"PANEL\\\"]\\n\\nPANEL.shape if PANEL is not None else None\"}",
      "serverName": "notebook_mcp"
    }
  ]
}

_**Agent (default)**_

Here is the distilled answer and what we changed.

### Answer

**You only need one coverage pass.** Those reports are built from **fixed inputs** (`mbb_df_player_box.csv`, `bpm_player_season_matched.csv`, `nbaplayersdraft.csv`, etc.). They do **not** use the analysis `perf` column or `perf_metric`. The only thing that changed when you swept metrics was **`export_plot_slug(cfg)` in the filenames**, so you were regenerating the **same** charts many times.

**Ventiles / LPM / (if enabled) data integrity** *do* depend on `perf` and `poolq_loo`, so those still need to run **per metric**.

### Change in your notebook

When **`RUN_ALL_PERF_METRICS`** is **True** and **`run_yearly_coverage_chart`** is **True**, CELL 4 now:

1. Runs **`dataset_coverage.export_yearly_coverage_chart(CFG, show=True)` once** right after **`prepare_panel`**
2. Sets **`run_yearly_coverage_chart=False`** on each **`run_conductor_from_panel`** in the loop

So you get a single set of coverage PNGs/CSVs per run. The slug in those names still comes from **CELL 2’s** `CFG` (`perf_metric` + z-score flag); that is only a **label** on the file, not different data.

A short note was also added to the **`export_yearly_coverage_chart`** docstring in `dataset_coverage.py` so this stays documented in code.

---

_**User**_

we need to trim all that extra space at the bottom of these plots, and please give me a boolean toggle for the metadata below that I asked for also

gs = fig.add_gridspec(2,1, height_ratios=[2,1])
fig.add_subplot(gs[0,0])
fig.add_subplot(gs[1,0])
fig.savefig('/tmp/t.png', bbox_inches='tight', pad_inches=0.1)
print('ok')
"
```

_**Agent (default)**_

Here is what changed.

### 1. **`ventile_plot_show_metadata` (`PipelineConfig`)**
- **`True` (default):** Same caption block as before (sample / draft / per-ventile *n*).
- **`False`:** PNG is **only** the main chart (title, axes, ventile line, optional LPM curve). No caption panel.

Full provenance is **unchanged** on disk and in the log: `run_ventile_eda` still writes **`ventile_eda_provenance_{slug}_{date}.txt`** and still prints that block to the console.

### 2. **Less empty space when metadata is on**
- Slightly **shorter** figure, **tighter** plot vs caption ratio (`height_ratios` 3.15 : 1.0 instead of 2.55 : 1.55).
- **`constrained_layout=True`** on the figure so spacing isn’t left oversized.
- **`fig.savefig(..., bbox_inches="tight", pad_inches=0.14)`** so extra white margin under the caption is trimmed (also applied when there is no caption for consistent framing).

### 3. **Notebook**
CELL 2 comments describe the flag, and **`ventile_plot_show_metadata=True`** is included in `PipelineConfig(...)`. For slides, set **`ventile_plot_show_metadata=False`** and keep the `.txt` file for the appendix or methods notes.

---

_**User**_

OK the bins are ventile (meaning what?), In my 520 code I would either bin by 'quantile' or 'equal_width'.  Can I get that same flexibility here?


---

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

The user wants to understand what "ventile" means in the current pipeline and wants flexibility similar to their 520 code - binning by either 'quantile' (which is what ventiles are - quantile-based bins) or 'equal_width' (equal width on the poolq_loo axis).

Let me read the ventile_table function and related code in panel_build.py to understand the current implementation, then add a config option for binning mode.
</details></think>

I need to:

1. **Explain ventile**: A ventile is one of 20 quantile-based bins (from Latin vicesimus). The code uses `pd.qcut` with `q=20` by default — equal **count** bins (approximately), i.e. **quantile** binning on `poolq_loo`. So "20 ventiles" = 20 quantile bins.

2. **Add `poolq_binning`** or `eda_poolq_bin_mode`: `"quantile"` | `"equal_width"` matching user's 520 code.

For **equal_width**: use `pd.cut` instead of `pd.qcut` on poolq_loo with `bins=q` (same number of bins).

Edge cases:
- `pd.cut` with duplicates at edges might create empty bins - use `pd.cut(..., duplicates='drop')` - actually cut doesn't have duplicates=, it's for qcut. For cut, we might get empty intervals - groupby will drop them with observed=True.
- Very skewed poolq might leave some equal-width bins empty — that's OK.

I'll add to config:
```python
#: How to bin ``poolq_loo`` for ventile EDA: ``quantile`` (~equal n per bin, legacy ``pd.qcut``)
#: or ``equal_width`` (``pd.cut`` on the min–max range of poolq).
poolq_binning: str = "quantile"  # or Literal
```

Validate in __post_init__ or in ventile_table: only allow "quantile", "equal_width".

Optional: include binning mode in `export_plot_slug` so filenames differ — user might run both. I'll add `_qcut` vs `_cut` or `quantile` vs `eqwidth` suffix to slug when not default. Actually that could clutter. Better add to slug always a short token: `export_plot_slug` could take optional extra - simpler to append in panel_build when writing CSV: 
`binned_draft_rate_bins_{mode}_{slug}_{stamp}.csv` 
or add to export_plot_slug in perf_metric.py:

Actually cleanest: extend `export_plot_slug(cfg)` to include binning when not quantile, e.g. `obpm_zwithinseason_eqwidth` vs default `obpm_zwithinseason` for quantile (backward compat: quantile doesn't add suffix).

Or always add `_pq` / `_ew` suffix for clarity — breaks existing filenames. User might prefer explicit: `poolq_binning_quantile` vs `poolq_binning_equal_width` short tokens `pq` and `ew`.

I'll add to `export_plot_slug` in perf_metric.py:
```python
def export_plot_slug(cfg: Any) -> str:
    ...
    bin_mode = str(getattr(cfg, "poolq_binning", "quantile")).strip().lower()
    if bin_mode not in ("quantile", "equal_width"):
        ...
    base = ...
    if bin_mode == "equal_width":
        base = f"{base}_poolqeqwidth"
    # quantile is default, no suffix for backward compat
```

Or always suffix: `_poolqq` and `_poolqew` for discoverability. I'll use `_qbin` omitted for quantile and `_ewbin` for equal_width to keep quantile filenames unchanged.

**ventile_plot title** says "20 ventiles" — when equal_width, misleading. Use generic "**N bins**" with subtitle of binning type.

Read ventile_provenance_lines for "Per-ventile n" text — update to "Per-bin n" and mention mode.

Read ventile_plot for title.

Implement `assign_poolq_bins` helper:

```python
def assign_poolq_bins(series: pd.Series, n_bins: int, mode: str) -> pd.Series:
    mode = mode.strip().lower()
    if mode == "quantile":
        return pd.qcut(series, q=n_bins, labels=False, duplicates="drop")
    if mode == "equal_width":
        return pd.cut(series, bins=n_bins, labels=False, duplicates="drop")
```

Wait: `pd.cut` doesn't have `duplicates` parameter in older pandas — that was added for qcut. For cut, if all values identical, might error. Check: `pd.cut([1,1,1], bins=3)` — might return NaN. We can use `pd.cut(..., include_lowest=True)`.

Actually pandas `pd.cut` signature: `bins` can be int - equal width. No duplicates kwarg in cut - I'll check pandas docs... In pandas 2.1+ maybe. For robustness:

```python
s = work["poolq_loo"]
if mode == "equal_width":
    try:
        work["vent"] = pd.cut(s, bins=q, labels=False, include_lowest=True)
    except ValueError:
        work["vent"] = 0
```

If constant poolq, cut fails — handle gracefully.

**LPM overlay** uses poolq mean per bin on x-axis - for equal_width, x positions are still poolq_mean per bin — good.

**CSV column** name `vent` is kept for backward compat or rename to `bin` — user might have scripts; keep `vent` as bin index.

Update config docstring for `ventiles` - still means "number of bins".

**pd.cut** with int bins: from min to max inclusive. If degenerate range, numpy may warn.

Test edge: all same poolq - pd.cut(..., bins=20) might return single bin or error. 
```python
>>> pd.Series([1.0]*100)
>>> pd.cut(..., bins=5)
ValueError: Bin edges must be unique
```
So we need try/except and fall back to single bin or quantile.

I'll wrap in ventile_table:

```python
def ventile_table(df: pd.DataFrame, cfg: Any) -> pd.DataFrame:
    q = int(getattr(cfg, "ventiles", 20))
    mode = str(getattr(cfg, "poolq_binning", "quantile")).strip().lower()
    work = df.copy()
    s = work["poolq_loo"]
    if mode == "equal_width":
        try:
            work["vent"] = pd.cut(s, bins=q, labels=False, include_lowest=True)
        except ValueError:
            work["vent"] = pd.qcut(s, q=q, labels=False, duplicates="drop")
    else:
        work["vent"] = pd.qcut(s, q=q, labels=False, duplicates="drop")
```

Actually if equal_width fails, falling back silently might confuse - better warn print and use qcut or single bin.

```python
        try:
            work["vent"] = pd.cut(s, bins=q, labels=False, include_lowest=True)
        except (ValueError, TypeError) as e:
            import warnings
            warnings.warn(
                f"poolq_binning=equal_width failed ({e!r}); falling back to quantile bins.",
                UserWarning,
            )
            work["vent"] = pd.qcut(s, q=q, labels=False, duplicates="drop")
```

**Duplicates in pd.cut**: In pandas 2.0+, `pd.cut` has parameter `duplicates='drop'` for the bins edge case... Let me check. Actually the issue is identical min=max.

{
  "result": "{\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"### Performance metrics (&#96;perf_metric&#96;) and z-scores\\n\\n**ESPN box (no SR merge)**\\n\\n- **&#96;minutes&#96;** — Player–season–team total minutes from game-level box. Leave-one-out (LOO) pool quality = mean teammate **minutes** excluding self.\\n- **&#96;ppm&#96;** — Points per minute from box. LOO = mean teammate **PPM** excluding self.\\n\\n**Sports Reference** (needs &#96;DO_NOT_ERASE/bpm_player_season_matched.csv&#96; on the panel)\\n\\n- **&#96;bpm&#96;** — Box Plus/Minus; SR all-in-one advanced impact statistic.\\n- **&#96;obpm&#96;** / **&#96;opm&#96;** — Offensive BPM (same matched row as BPM).\\n- **&#96;dbpm&#96;** / **&#96;dpm&#96;** — Defensive BPM.\\n- **&#96;per&#96;** — Player efficiency rating; composite efficiency index.\\n- **&#96;ws40&#96;** — Win shares **per 40 minutes** (rate).\\n- **&#96;ws&#96;** — Win **shares** (season total at the matched row).\\n- **&#96;tspct&#96;** or **&#96;ts_pct&#96;** — True shooting **percentage** from SR (panel column &#96;ts_pct_sr&#96;).\\n\\n**&#96;perf_zscore_within_season&#96;** — If &#96;True&#96;, &#96;perf&#96; is replaced by its **z-score within each collegiate season** before LOO. Then &#96;poolq_loo&#96; is in **within-season SD units** (useful for comparing metrics or multi-metric plots later).\\n\\n**Export filenames** — Figures and CSVs under &#96;CFG.exports_dir&#96; include a slug from &#96;perf_metric&#96; plus &#96;_zwithinseason&#96; when z-scoring is on, e.g. &#96;inverted_u_ventiles_dbpm_2026-04-01.png&#96; vs &#96;dataset_coverage_dashboard_obpm_zwithinseason_2026-04-01.png&#96;, so each PowerPoint slide can match a distinct file.\\n\\n### Unmatched schools (CELL 2 toggles)\\n\\n- **Draft** — &#96;print_unmatched_school_lists=True&#96; prints colleges from the NBA draft register that never mapped to your ESPN school linkage. Source QA file: &#96;datasets/mbb/draft_unmapped_colleges.csv&#96; (from your draft-match export).\\n- **Sports Reference** — Same toggle prints ESPN **teams** (with &#96;team_id&#96;, display name, &#96;school_slug&#96;) that still have player-season rows without a BPM merge hit after the last &#96;run_match&#96;. Source: &#96;datasets/mbb/bpm_panel_rows_unmatched.csv&#96;. Small religious colleges, HBCUs, NAIA, etc. often show up here when SR slugs or rosters do not line up—useful for planning manual aliases, conference-level imputation, or alternate pool-quality proxies.\"}],\"structuredContent\":{\"result\":\"### Performance metrics (&#96;perf_metric&#96;) and z-scores\\n\\n**ESPN box (no SR merge)**\\n\\n- **&#96;minutes&#96;** — Player–season–team total minutes from game-level box. Leave-one-out (LOO) pool quality = mean teammate **minutes** excluding self.\\n- **&#96;ppm&#96;** — Points per minute from box. LOO = mean teammate **PPM** excluding self.\\n\\n**Sports Reference** (needs &#96;DO_NOT_ERASE/bpm_player_season_matched.csv&#96; on the panel)\\n\\n- **&#96;bpm&#96;** — Box Plus/Minus; SR all-in-one advanced impact statistic.\\n- **&#96;obpm&#96;** / **&#96;opm&#96;** — Offensive BPM (same matched row as BPM).\\n- **&#96;dbpm&#96;** / **&#96;dpm&#96;** — Defensive BPM.\\n- **&#96;per&#96;** — Player efficiency rating; composite efficiency index.\\n- **&#96;ws40&#96;** — Win shares **per 40 minutes** (rate).\\n- **&#96;ws&#96;** — Win **shares** (season total at the matched row).\\n- **&#96;tspct&#96;** or **&#96;ts_pct&#96;** — True shooting **percentage** from SR (panel column &#96;ts_pct_sr&#96;).\\n\\n**&#96;perf_zscore_within_season&#96;** — If &#96;True&#96;, &#96;perf&#96; is replaced by its **z-score within each collegiate season** before LOO. Then &#96;poolq_loo&#96; is in **within-season SD units** (useful for comparing metrics or multi-metric plots later).\\n\\n**Export filenames** — Figures and CSVs under &#96;CFG.exports_dir&#96; include a slug from &#96;perf_metric&#96; plus &#96;_zwithinseason&#96; when z-scoring is on, e.g. &#96;inverted_u_ventiles_dbpm_2026-04-01.png&#96; vs &#96;dataset_coverage_dashboard_obpm_zwithinseason_2026-04-01.png&#96;, so each PowerPoint slide can match a distinct file.\\n\\n### Unmatched schools (CELL 2 toggles)\\n\\n- **Draft** — &#96;print_unmatched_school_lists=True&#96; prints colleges from the NBA draft register that never mapped to your ESPN school linkage. Source QA file: &#96;datasets/mbb/draft_unmapped_colleges.csv&#96; (from your draft-match export).\\n- **Sports Reference** — Same toggle prints ESPN **teams** (with &#96;team_id&#96;, display name, &#96;school_slug&#96;) that still have player-season rows without a BPM merge hit after the last &#96;run_match&#96;. Source: &#96;datasets/mbb/bpm_panel_rows_unmatched.csv&#96;. Small religious colleges, HBCUs, NAIA, etc. often show up here when SR slugs or rosters do not line up—useful for planning manual aliases, conference-level imputation, or alternate pool-quality proxies.\"}}"
}

Parameters:

```json
{
  "tools": [
    {
      "name": "notebook_edit_cell",
      "parameters": "{\"notebook_path\":\"530_sports_pipeline.ipynb\",\"cell_index\":3,\"source\":\"### Performance metrics (&#96;perf_metric&#96;) and z-scores\\n\\n**ESPN box (no SR merge)**\\n\\n- **&#96;minutes&#96;** — Player–season–team total minutes from game-level box. Leave-one-out (LOO) pool quality = mean teammate **minutes** excluding self.\\n- **&#96;ppm&#96;** — Points per minute from box. LOO = mean teammate **PPM** excluding self.\\n\\n**Sports Reference** (needs &#96;DO_NOT_ERASE/bpm_player_season_matched.csv&#96; on the panel)\\n\\n- **&#96;bpm&#96;** — Box Plus/Minus; SR all-in-one advanced impact statistic.\\n- **&#96;obpm&#96;** / **&#96;opm&#96;** — Offensive BPM (same matched row as BPM).\\n- **&#96;dbpm&#96;** / **&#96;dpm&#96;** — Defensive BPM.\\n- **&#96;per&#96;** — Player efficiency rating; composite efficiency index.\\n- **&#96;ws40&#96;** — Win shares **per 40 minutes** (rate).\\n- **&#96;ws&#96;** — Win **shares** (season total at the matched row).\\n- **&#96;tspct&#96;** or **&#96;ts_pct&#96;** — True shooting **percentage** from SR (panel column &#96;ts_pct_sr&#96;).\\n\\n**&#96;perf_zscore_within_season&#96;** — If &#96;True&#96;, &#96;perf&#96; is replaced by its **z-score within each collegiate season** before LOO. Then &#96;poolq_loo&#96; is in **within-season SD units** (useful for comparing metrics or multi-metric plots later).\\n\\n### Bins on &#96;poolq_loo&#96; (&#96;ventiles&#96; + &#96;poolq_binning&#96;)\\n\\n- **&#96;ventiles&#96;** — Number of bins (default 20). The plot title says **ventiles** only when &#96;ventiles=20&#96; **and** &#96;poolq_binning=\\\"quantile\\\"&#96;: that is the usual “20 quantile groups,” i.e. ~5% of the sample per bin.\\n- **&#96;poolq_binning=\\\"quantile\\\"&#96;** — &#96;pd.qcut&#96; on &#96;poolq_loo&#96; (~equal **counts** per bin). Matches a 520-style **quantile** binning choice.\\n- **&#96;poolq_binning=\\\"equal_width\\\"&#96;** — &#96;pd.cut&#96; on the min–max range of &#96;poolq_loo&#96; (equal **width** on the x-axis). Matches 520 **equal_width**. Export filenames add &#96;_poolqeqwidth&#96; so you do not overwrite quantile runs.\\n\\n**Export filenames** — Slugs include &#96;perf_metric&#96;, optional &#96;_zwithinseason&#96;, and optional &#96;_poolqeqwidth&#96; for equal-width bins.\\n\\n### Unmatched schools (CELL 2 toggles)\\n\\n- **Draft** — &#96;print_unmatched_school_lists=True&#96; prints colleges from the NBA draft register that never mapped to your ESPN school linkage. Source QA file: &#96;datasets/mbb/draft_unmapped_colleges.csv&#96; (from your draft-match export).\\n- **Sports Reference** — Same toggle prints ESPN **teams** (with &#96;team_id&#96;, display name, &#96;school_slug&#96;) that still have player-season rows without a BPM merge hit after the last &#96;run_match&#96;. Source: &#96;datasets/mbb/bpm_panel_rows_unmatched.csv&#96;. Small religious colleges, HBCUs, NAIA, etc. often show up here when SR slugs or rosters do not line up—useful for planning manual aliases, conference-level imputation, or alternate pool-quality proxies.\"}",
      "serverName": "notebook_mcp"
    }
  ]
}

_**Agent (default)**_

Here is the concise picture and what was implemented.

### What “ventile” meant here

A **ventile** is one of **20 quantile groups** on `poolq_loo`: each bin holds about **1/20 of the sample** (same idea as `pd.qcut`). Your code already did that when `ventiles=20`; the word “ventile” is just the 1/20 case of **quantile** binning.

### 520-style flexibility

New config field:

- **`poolq_binning="quantile"`** (default) — `pd.qcut` on `poolq_loo` → ~**equal counts** per bin (your old “ventiles” when `ventiles=20`).
- **`poolq_binning="equal_width"`** — `pd.cut` over **[min, max]** `poolq_loo` → **equal-width** intervals on the x-axis (520 **equal_width**). Counts per bin can be very unequal.

**`ventiles`** is still the **number of bins** (not only 20).

If `equal_width` fails (e.g. almost constant `poolq_loo`), the code **warns** and **falls back** to quantile bins.

### Filenames

`export_plot_slug` adds **`_poolqeqwidth`** when `poolq_binning="equal_width"` so quantile and equal-width exports do not overwrite each other (e.g. `obpm_poolqeqwidth_zwithinseason_…`).

### Code / notebook

- `assign_poolq_bin_labels` + updated `ventile_table` in `panel_build.py`
- Titles and provenance text describe the mode; bin index **`vent`** is still 0 = **lowest** `poolq_loo`
- `PipelineConfig.poolq_binning` with validation in `__post_init__`
- CELL 2 + glossary markdown updated

The **LPM overlay** is still fit on **continuous** `poolq_loo` in the full sample; only the **binned draft-rate** points change with binning mode.

---
