```json
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 827,
            "text": "def plot_competing_risks_cif_bars("
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 828,
            "text": "    plot_df,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 829,
            "text": "    plot_spec,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 830,
            "text": "    metadata,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 831,
            "text": "    output_dir=None,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 832,
            "text": "    filename_base=None,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 833,
            "text": "    dpi=300,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 834,
            "text": "    figsize=(15, 10),"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 835,
            "text": "    legend_outside=True,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 836,
            "text": "):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 837,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 838,
            "text": "    Plot bar charts of final CIF by bin for promotion and attrition."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 839
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 840,
            "text": "    Bars are ordered by the mean of the plotted variable (ascending),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 841,
            "text": "    which yields z-score ascending order when plotting z-scored variables."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 842,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 843,
            "text": "    if plot_df is None or plot_df.empty:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 844,
            "text": "        print(\"⚠️ No data for CIF bar plots.\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 845,
            "text": "        return None"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 846
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 847,
            "text": "    var_name = plot_spec.get(\"variable\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 848,
            "text": "    group_col = \"plot_group\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 849,
            "text": "    if group_col not in plot_df.columns:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 850,
            "text": "        print(\"⚠️ plot_group missing; cannot build CIF bars.\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 851,
            "text": "        return None"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 852
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 853,
            "text": "    # Compute per-group stats"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 854,
            "text": "    group_stats = []"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 855,
            "text": "    for group, gdf in plot_df.groupby(group_col):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 856,
            "text": "        # Match competing-risks filtering: require valid stop time"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 857,
            "text": "        if \"stop_time\" in gdf.columns:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 858,
            "text": "            gdf = gdf[gdf[\"stop_time\"].notna() \u0026 (gdf[\"stop_time\"] \u003e 0)].copy()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 859,
            "text": "        n = len(gdf)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 860,
            "text": "        if n == 0:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 861,
            "text": "            continue"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 862,
            "text": "        promo_rate = (gdf[\"event\"] == 1).sum() / n if \"event\" in gdf.columns else np.nan"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 863,
            "text": "        attr_rate = (gdf[\"event\"] == 2).sum() / n if \"event\" in gdf.columns else np.nan"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 864,
            "text": "        if var_name and var_name in gdf.columns:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 865,
            "text": "            mean_val = pd.to_numeric(gdf[var_name], errors=\"coerce\").mean()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 866,
            "text": "            min_val = pd.to_numeric(gdf[var_name], errors=\"coerce\").min()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 867,
            "text": "            max_val = pd.to_numeric(gdf[var_name], errors=\"coerce\").max()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 868,
            "text": "        else:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 869,
            "text": "            mean_val = min_val = max_val = np.nan"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 870,
            "text": "        group_stats.append({"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 871,
            "text": "            \"group\": group,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 872,
            "text": "            \"n\": n,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 873,
            "text": "            \"promo_rate\": promo_rate,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 874,
            "text": "            \"attr_rate\": attr_rate,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 875,
            "text": "            \"mean_val\": mean_val,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 876,
            "text": "            \"min_val\": min_val,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 877,
            "text": "            \"max_val\": max_val,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 878,
            "text": "        })"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 879
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 880,
            "text": "    if not group_stats:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 881,
            "text": "        print(\"⚠️ No group stats for CIF bar plots.\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 882,
            "text": "        return None"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 883
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 884,
            "text": "    # Sort by mean of variable (ascending). Fallback to group label order."
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 1012
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "column": 22,
            "line": 823
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./zips/TALENT_NET_export_20260305-0835/cox_plot_helpers.py",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.32637929916381836
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 446,
            "text": "def analyze_competing_risks(officer_data, group_col, title_suffix):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 447,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 448,
            "text": "    TRANSFORMATION 2: Competing Risks Analysis"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 449,
            "text": "    "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 450,
            "text": "    Shows the different ways officers can exit the promotion process:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 451,
            "text": "    - Promoted to Major (success)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 452,
            "text": "    - Left Army (attrition - competing risk)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 453,
            "text": "    - Administrative censoring (still in process)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 454,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 455,
            "text": "    print(f\"📊 Creating Competing Risks Analysis {title_suffix}\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 456,
            "text": "    "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 457,
            "text": "    # Create competing risks visualization"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 458,
            "text": "    fig, ((ax1, ax2), (ax3, ax4)) = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(16, 12))"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 459,
            "text": "    "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 460,
            "text": "    # Plot 1: Cumulative Incidence by Outcome Type"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 461,
            "text": "    groups = officer_data[group_col].unique()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 462,
            "text": "    colors = plt.cm.Set1(np.linspace(0, 1, len(groups)))"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 463,
            "text": "    "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 464,
            "text": "    for i, group in enumerate(groups):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 465,
            "text": "        group_data = officer_data[officer_data[group_col] == group]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 466,
            "text": "        "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 467,
            "text": "        if len(group_data) == 0:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 468,
            "text": "            continue"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 469,
            "text": "            "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 470,
            "text": "        # Calculate cumulative incidence"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 471,
            "text": "        promoted = group_data['event'].sum()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 472,
            "text": "        total = len(group_data)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 473,
            "text": "        promotion_rate = promoted / total"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 474,
            "text": "        "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 475,
            "text": "        # Simple bar chart for now (can be enhanced with time-dependent analysis)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 476,
            "text": "        ax1.bar([f'Group {group}'], [promotion_rate], color=colors[i], alpha=0.7)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 477,
            "text": "    "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 478,
            "text": "    ax1.set_title(f'Promotion Rates by Group {title_suffix}')"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 479,
            "text": "    ax1.set_ylabel('Promotion Rate')"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 480,
            "text": "    ax1.set_ylim(0, 1)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 481,
            "text": "    "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 482,
            "text": "    # Plot 2: Event Distribution"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 483,
            "text": "    event_counts = officer_data.groupby([group_col, 'event']).size().unstack(fill_value=0)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 484,
            "text": "    event_counts.plot(kind='bar', ax=ax2, color=['red', 'green'])"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 485,
            "text": "    ax2.set_title(f'Event Distribution {title_suffix}')"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 486,
            "text": "    ax2.set_ylabel('Number of Officers')"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 487,
            "text": "    ax2.legend(['Not Promoted', 'Promoted'])"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 488,
            "text": "    ax2.tick_params(axis='x', rotation=45)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 489,
            "text": "    "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 490,
            "text": "    # Plot 3: Time to Event Distribution"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 491,
            "text": "    for i, group in enumerate(groups):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 492,
            "text": "        group_data = officer_data[officer_data[group_col] == group]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 493,
            "text": "        if len(group_data) \u003e 0:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 494,
            "text": "            ax3.hist(group_data['survival_days'], alpha=0.6, label=f'Group {group}', "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 495,
            "text": "                    color=colors[i], bins=20)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 496,
            "text": "    "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 497,
            "text": "    ax3.set_title(f'Time to Event Distribution {title_suffix}')"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 531
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "column": 14,
            "line": 443
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./1-Various_PDE_and_Chat_stuff/2-cox_analysis/cox_flag.py",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.3255806863307953
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 972,
            "text": "    'plots': ["
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 973,
            "text": "        # Add plot specifications here"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 974,
            "text": "        # Each plot is a dictionary with required keys:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 975,
            "text": "        #   - 'name': str (unique identifier, used internally and in metadata)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 976,
            "text": "        #              NOTE: Actual filename is auto-generated with enhanced description"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 977,
            "text": "        #   - 'variable': str or None (column name to analyze, must be in COLUMN_CONFIG, or None for grouping only)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 978,
            "text": "        #   - 'group_by': str or None (column name to group by, creates separate curves, or None)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 979,
            "text": "        #   - 'plot_type': str ('kaplan_meier' or 'competing_risks')"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 980,
            "text": "        #   - 'bin_continuous': bool (True for continuous variables, False for categorical)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 981,
            "text": "        #   - 'n_bins': int (number of bins, ignored if bin_continuous=False)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 982,
            "text": "        #   - 'bin_method': str ('quantile' or 'equal_width', ignored if bin_continuous=False)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 983,
            "text": "        #   - 'min_group_size': int (minimum officers per group)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 984,
            "text": "        #   - 'use_time_varying': bool (True for time-varying vars, False for static, default True)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 985,
            "text": "        #   - 'filter_zero_oer': bool (True to exclude officers with no OER data, only for OER variables)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 986,
            "text": "        #   - 'filter_nan_variable': bool (True to drop rows where variable is NaN)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 987,
            "text": "        #   - 'filter_nan_group_by': bool (True to drop rows where group_by is NaN)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 988,
            "text": "        #   - 'strict_equal_n': bool (force equal-N bins with rank-based qcut)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 989,
            "text": "        #   - 'x_max_days': int or None (cap x-axis at N days)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 990,
            "text": "        #   - 'bin_group_by': bool (True to bin group_by separately)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 991,
            "text": "        #   - 'group_bins': int (number of bins for group_by, defaults to n_bins)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 992,
            "text": "        #   - 'group_bin_method': str ('quantile' or 'equal_width' for group_by)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 993,
            "text": "        #"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 994,
            "text": "        # OUTPUT FILES:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 995,
            "text": "        #   - Plot: {plot_type}_{variable}_{group_by}_{bins}_{method}_{filter}_{dates}.png"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 996,
            "text": "        #   - Metadata: {plot_type}_{variable}_{group_by}_{bins}_{method}_{filter}_{dates}_metadata.json"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 997,
            "text": "        ########################################################################################################################################"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 998,
            "text": "        {    # 1: TB ratio (mode-specific, raw)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 999,
            "text": "            'variable': TB_RATIO_SNR_COL,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1000,
            "text": "            'name': 'cr_' + TB_RATIO_SNR_COL,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1001,
            "text": "            'group_by': None,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1002,
            "text": "            'plot_type': 'competing_risks',"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1003,
            "text": "            'bin_continuous': True,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1004,
            "text": "            'n_bins': 5,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1005,
            "text": "            'bin_method': 'quantile',"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1006,
            "text": "            'min_group_size': 3,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1007,
            "text": "            'use_time_varying': True,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1008,

          },

      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 934,
            "text": "def plot_competing_risks_cif_bars("
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 935,
            "text": "    plot_df,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 936,
            "text": "    plot_spec,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 937,
            "text": "    metadata,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 938,
            "text": "    output_dir=None,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 939,
            "text": "    filename_base=None,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 940,
            "text": "    dpi=400,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 941,
            "text": "    figsize=(10, 9),"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 942,
            "text": "    tick_sz=None,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 943,
            "text": "    legend_outside=True,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 944,
            "text": "):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1008
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1009,
            "text": "    footnote_lines = []"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1010,
            "text": "    for gs in group_stats:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1011,
            "text": "        labels.append(str(gs[\"group\"]))"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1012,
            "text": "        promo_vals.append(gs[\"promo_rate\"])"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1013,
            "text": "        attr_vals.append(gs[\"attr_rate\"])"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1014,
            "text": "        legend_labels.append(str(gs[\"group\"]))"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1015,
            "text": "        if not np.isnan(gs[\"mean_val\"]):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1016,
            "text": "            footnote_lines.append("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1017,
            "text": "                f\"{gs['group']}: N={gs['n']:,}, avg={gs['mean_val']:.3f}, \""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1018,
            "text": "                f\"range={gs['min_val']:.3f}-{gs['max_val']:.3f}\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1019,
            "text": "            )"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1020,
            "text": "        else:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1021,
            "text": "            footnote_lines.append(f\"{gs['group']}: N={gs['n']:,}\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1022,
            "text": "    footnote_lines = []"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1023,
            "text": "    fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 1, figsize=figsize, sharex=False)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1024,
            "text": "    ax1, ax2 = axes"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1025,
            "text": "    x = np.arange(len(labels))"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1026,
            "text": "    ordered_groups = [gs[\"group\"] for gs in group_stats]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1027,
            "text": "    n_ord = len(ordered_groups)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1028,
            "text": "    q_labels = [f\"Q{i+1}\" for i in range(n_ord)]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1029
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1030,
            "text": "    xtick_mode = plot_spec.get(\"cif_bar_xtick_labels\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1031,
            "text": "    if xtick_mode is None:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1032,
            "text": "        xtick_mode = ("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1033,
            "text": "            \"group\" if (plot_spec.get(\"group_only_curves\") and plot_spec.get(\"group_by\")) else \"quantile\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1034,
            "text": "        )"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1035,
            "text": "    if xtick_mode == \"group\":"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1036,
            "text": "        bar_cats = [str(gs[\"group\"]) for gs in group_stats]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1037,
            "text": "    else:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1038,
            "text": "        bar_cats = list(q_labels)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1039
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1040,
            "text": "    def _hex_to_rgb(hex_color):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1041,
            "text": "        hex_color = hex_color.lstrip(\"#\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1042,
            "text": "        return tuple(int(hex_color[i:i+2], 16) / 255.0 for i in (0, 2, 4))"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1043
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1044,
            "text": "    def _rgb_to_hex(rgb):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1045,
            "text": "        return \"#%02x%02x%02x\" % tuple(int(round(c * 255)) for c in rgb)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1046
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1047,
            "text": "    def _build_hex_gradient(hex_colors, n_colors):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1048,
            "text": "        if not hex_colors:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1049,
            "text": "            return None"
          }
        ],

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 832,
            "text": "def plot_competing_risks_cif_bars("
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 833,
            "text": "    plot_df,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 834,
            "text": "    plot_spec,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 835,
            "text": "    metadata,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 836,
            "text": "    output_dir=None,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 837,
            "text": "    filename_base=None,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 838,
            "text": "    dpi=300,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 839,
            "text": "    figsize=(10, 9),"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 840,
            "text": "    tick_sz=None,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 841,
            "text": "    legend_outside=True,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 842,
            "text": "):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 843,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 844,
            "text": "    Plot bar charts of final CIF by bin for promotion and attrition."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 845
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 846,
            "text": "    Bars are ordered by the mean of the plotted variable (ascending),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 847,
            "text": "    which yields z-score ascending order when plotting z-scored variables."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 848,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 849,
            "text": "    if plot_df is None or plot_df.empty:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 850,
            "text": "        print(\"⚠️ No data for CIF bar plots.\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 851,
            "text": "        return None"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 852
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 853,
            "text": "    var_name = plot_spec.get(\"variable\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 854,
            "text": "    group_col = \"plot_group\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 855,
            "text": "    if group_col not in plot_df.columns:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 856,
            "text": "        print(\"⚠️ plot_group missing; cannot build CIF bars.\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 857,
            "text": "        return None"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 858
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 859,
            "text": "    # Compute per-group stats"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 860,
            "text": "    group_stats = []"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 861,
            "text": "    for group, gdf in plot_df.groupby(group_col):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 862,
            "text": "        # Match competing-risks filtering: require valid stop time"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 863,
            "text": "        if \"stop_time\" in gdf.columns:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 864,
            "text": "            gdf = gdf[gdf[\"stop_time\"].notna() \u0026 (gdf[\"stop_time\"] \u003e 0)].copy()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 865,
            "text": "        n = len(gdf)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 866,
            "text": "        if n == 0:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 867,
            "text": "            continue"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 868,
            "text": "        promo_rate = (gdf[\"event\"] == 1).sum() / n if \"event\" in gdf.columns else np.nan"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 869,
            "text": "        attr_rate = (gdf[\"event\"] == 2).sum() / n if \"event\" in gdf.columns else np.nan"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 870,
            "text": "        if var_name and var_name in gdf.columns:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 871,
            "text": "            mean_val = pd.to_numeric(gdf[var_name], errors=\"coerce\").mean()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 872,
            "text": "            min_val = pd.to_numeric(gdf[var_name], errors=\"coerce\").min()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 873,
            "text": "            max_val = pd.to_numeric(gdf[var_name], errors=\"coerce\").max()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 874,
            "text": "        else:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 875,
            "text": "            mean_val = min_val = max_val = np.nan"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 876,
            "text": "        group_stats.append({"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 877,
            "text": "            \"group\": group,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 878,
            "text": "            \"n\": n,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 879,
            "text": "            \"promo_rate\": promo_rate,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 880,
            "text": "            \"attr_rate\": attr_rate,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 881,
            "text": "            \"mean_val\": mean_val,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 882,
            "text": "            \"min_val\": min_val,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 883,
            "text": "            \"max_val\": max_val,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 884,
            "text": "        })"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 885
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 886,
            "text": "    if not group_stats:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 887,
            "text": "        print(\"⚠️ No group stats for CIF bar plots.\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 888,
            "text": "        return None"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 889
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 890,
            "text": "    # Sort by mean of variable (ascending). Fallback to group label order."
          }
        ],
```

**Comparing ventile plots**

I'm comparing the current ventile plot implementation with the reference notebook to ensure consistent visualization. 

**Updating ventile plots**

I'm implementing the 520-style bar chart pattern with bars by bin index and line vs median overlay. I'm adding poolq_median to ventile_table for faithful 520 bar chart implementation. 

I'm adding `poolq_median` to ventile_table's groupby aggregation, which will affect CSV output but aligns with user's request for bin-based plots. I'm adding poolq_median data to the right panel and implementing the dual-panel layout with title split and foot caption. I'm implementing quadratic overlay on right panel and handling provenance layout with tight_layout and fig.text for metadata. I'm implementing a hybrid layout for provenance display that uses gridspec for multi-panel plots and fig.text for metadata when needed. I'm adjusting the layout to use gridspec with a 3-row structure for better provenance display consistency. 

I'm adjusting the plot titles to include bin_mode and adding a performance measure text annotation. I'm refactoring the plot layout using GridSpec for better control over subplot arrangement. </details></think>

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd "/Users/charleslevine/Library/CloudStorage/Dropbox/1-Documents/00- Dissertation/0-Next_Chapter/Code_and_Data/New SQL and PY Code/Cursor Workspace PDE" && python -c "
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from sports_pipeline.config import PipelineConfig
from sports_pipeline.panel_build import ventile_table, ventile_plot

np.random.seed(0)
n = 800
df = pd.DataFrame({
    'poolq_loo': np.random.randn(n),
    'poolq_sq': np.random.randn(n)**2,
    'Y_draft': (np.random.rand(n) > 0.92).astype(int),
    'athlete_id': np.arange(n),
    'season': np.random.randint(2010, 2020, n),
    'team_id': np.random.randint(1, 50, n),
    'minutes': np.random.rand(n) * 500,
})
cfg = PipelineConfig(ventile_eda_plot_style='bins_bars_520', restrict_teams_by_draftees=False)
bt = ventile_table(df, cfg)
assert 'poolq_median' in bt.columns
p = ventile_plot(bt, cfg, n, use=df, provenance_lines=[], show=False)
print(p.name)
cfg2 = PipelineConfig(ventile_eda_plot_style='poolq_line', restrict_teams_by_draftees=False)
p2 = ventile_plot(bt, cfg2, n, use=df, provenance_lines=[], show=False)
print(p2.name)
print('ok')
"
</summary>

```bash
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from sports_pipeline.config import PipelineConfig
from sports_pipeline.panel_build import ventile_table, ventile_plot

np.random.seed(0)
n = 800
df = pd.DataFrame({
    'poolq_loo': np.random.randn(n),
    'poolq_sq': np.random.randn(n)**2,
    'Y_draft': (np.random.rand(n) > 0.92).astype(int),
    'athlete_id': np.arange(n),
    'season': np.random.randint(2010, 2020, n),
    'team_id': np.random.randint(1, 50, n),
    'minutes': np.random.rand(n) * 500,
})
cfg = PipelineConfig(ventile_eda_plot_style='bins_bars_520', restrict_teams_by_draftees=False)
bt = ventile_table(df, cfg)
assert 'poolq_median' in bt.columns
p = ventile_plot(bt, cfg, n, use=df, provenance_lines=[], show=False)
print(p.name)
cfg2 = PipelineConfig(ventile_eda_plot_style='poolq_line', restrict_teams_by_draftees=False)
p2 = ventile_plot(bt, cfg2, n, use=df, provenance_lines=[], show=False)
print(p2.name)
print('ok')
"
```

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from sports_pipeline.config import PipelineConfig
from sports_pipeline.panel_build import ventile_table, ventile_plot, ventile_provenance_lines

np.random.seed(0)
n = 800
df = pd.DataFrame({
    'poolq_loo': np.random.randn(n),
    'poolq_sq': np.random.randn(n)**2,
    'Y_draft': (np.random.rand(n) > 0.92).astype(int),
    'athlete_id': np.arange(n),
    'season': np.random.randint(2010, 2020, n),
    'team_id': np.random.randint(1, 50, n),
    'minutes': np.random.rand(n) * 500,
})
cfg = PipelineConfig(ventile_eda_plot_style='bins_bars_520', restrict_teams_by_draftees=False, ventile_plot_show_metadata=True)
bt = ventile_table(df, cfg)
prov = ventile_provenance_lines(df, cfg, bt)
ventile_plot(bt, cfg, n, use=df, provenance_lines=prov, show=False)
print('ok')
"

```bash
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from sports_pipeline.config import PipelineConfig
from sports_pipeline.panel_build import ventile_table, ventile_plot, ventile_provenance_lines

np.random.seed(0)
n = 800
df = pd.DataFrame({
    'poolq_loo': np.random.randn(n),
    'poolq_sq': np.random.randn(n)**2,
    'Y_draft': (np.random.rand(n) > 0.92).astype(int),
    'athlete_id': np.arange(n),
    'season': np.random.randint(2010, 2020, n),
    'team_id': np.random.randint(1, 50, n),
    'minutes': np.random.rand(n) * 500,
})
cfg = PipelineConfig(ventile_eda_plot_style='bins_bars_520', restrict_teams_by_draftees=False, ventile_plot_show_metadata=True)
bt = ventile_table(df, cfg)
prov = ventile_provenance_lines(df, cfg, bt)
ventile_plot(bt, cfg, n, use=df, provenance_lines=prov, show=False)
print('ok')
"
```

```json
{
  "result": "{\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"### Performance metrics (&#96;perf_metric&#96;) and z-scores\\n\\n**CELL 2** sets **&#96;perf_metric&#96;** to a **list** of keys (e.g. &#96;[\\\"obpm\\\"]&#96; or &#96;[\\\"obpm\\\", \\\"ws40\\\"]&#96;). A one-shot &#96;run_conductor&#96; uses the **first** entry; listing several keys triggers a sweep in **CELL 4** (prepare once, then one analysis pass per key). Duplicates are dropped at init (case-insensitive).\\n\\n**ESPN box (no SR merge)**\\n\\n- **&#96;minutes&#96;** — Player–season–team total minutes from game-level box. Leave-one-out (LOO) pool quality = mean teammate **minutes** excluding self.\\n- **&#96;ppm&#96;** — Points per minute from box. LOO = mean teammate **PPM** excluding self.\\n\\n**Sports Reference** (needs &#96;DO_NOT_ERASE/bpm_player_season_matched.csv&#96; on the panel)\\n\\n- **&#96;bpm&#96;** — Box Plus/Minus; SR all-in-one advanced impact statistic.\\n- **&#96;obpm&#96;** / **&#96;opm&#96;** — Offensive BPM (same matched row as BPM).\\n- **&#96;dbpm&#96;** / **&#96;dpm&#96;** — Defensive BPM.\\n- **&#96;per&#96;** — Player efficiency rating; composite efficiency index.\\n- **&#96;ws40&#96;** — Win shares **per 40 minutes** (rate).\\n- **&#96;ws&#96;** — Win **shares** (season total at the matched row).\\n- **&#96;tspct&#96;** or **&#96;ts_pct&#96;** — True shooting **percentage** from SR (panel column &#96;ts_pct_sr&#96;).\\n\\n**&#96;perf_zscore_within_season&#96;** — If &#96;True&#96;, &#96;perf&#96; is replaced by its **z-score within each collegiate season** before LOO. Then &#96;poolq_loo&#96; is in **within-season SD units** (useful for comparing metrics or multi-metric plots later).\\n\\n### Bins on &#96;poolq_loo&#96; (&#96;ventiles&#96; + &#96;poolq_binning&#96;)\\n\\n- **&#96;ventiles&#96;** — Number of bins (default 20). The plot title says **ventiles** only when &#96;ventiles=20&#96; **and** &#96;poolq_binning=\\\"quantile\\\"&#96;: that is the usual “20 quantile groups,” i.e. ~5% of the sample per bin.\\n- **&#96;poolq_binning=\\\"quantile\\\"&#96;** — &#96;pd.qcut&#96; on &#96;poolq_loo&#96; (~equal **counts** per bin). Matches a 520-style **quantile** binning choice.\\n- **&#96;poolq_binning=\\\"equal_width\\\"&#96;** — &#96;pd.cut&#96; on the min–max range of &#96;poolq_loo&#96; (equal **width** on the x-axis). Matches 520 **equal_width**. Export filenames add &#96;_poolqeqwidth&#96; so you do not overwrite quantile runs.\\n\\n**Export filenames** — Slugs use the **active** perf key (first in the list for that run), plus optional &#96;_zwithinseason&#96;, and optional &#96;_poolqeqwidth&#96; for equal-width bins.\\n\\n### Unmatched schools (CELL 2 toggles)\\n\\n- **Draft** — &#96;print_unmatched_school_lists=True&#96; prints colleges from the NBA draft register that never mapped to your ESPN school linkage. Source QA file: &#96;datasets/mbb/draft_unmapped_colleges.csv&#96; (from your draft-match export).\\n- **Sports Reference** — Same toggle prints ESPN **teams** (with &#96;team_id&#96;, display name, &#96;school_slug&#96;) that still have player-season rows without a BPM merge hit after the last &#96;run_match&#96;. Source: &#96;datasets/mbb/bpm_panel_rows_unmatched.csv&#96;. Small religious colleges, HBCUs, NAIA, etc. often show up here when SR slugs or rosters do not line up—useful for planning manual aliases, conference-level imputation, or alternate pool-quality proxies.\"}],\"structuredContent\":{\"result\":\"### Performance metrics (&#96;perf_metric&#96;) and z-scores\\n\\n**CELL 2** sets **&#96;perf_metric&#96;** to a **list** of keys (e.g. &#96;[\\\"obpm\\\"]&#96; or &#96;[\\\"obpm\\\", \\\"ws40\\\"]&#96;). A one-shot &#96;run_conductor&#96; uses the **first** entry; listing several keys triggers a sweep in **CELL 4** (prepare once, then one analysis pass per key). Duplicates are dropped at init (case-insensitive).\\n\\n**ESPN box (no SR merge)**\\n\\n- **&#96;minutes&#96;** — Player–season–team total minutes from game-level box. Leave-one-out (LOO) pool quality = mean teammate **minutes** excluding self.\\n- **&#96;ppm&#96;** — Points per minute from box. LOO = mean teammate **PPM** excluding self.\\n\\n**Sports Reference** (needs &#96;DO_NOT_ERASE/bpm_player_season_matched.csv&#96; on the panel)\\n\\n- **&#96;bpm&#96;** — Box Plus/Minus; SR all-in-one advanced impact statistic.\\n- **&#96;obpm&#96;** / **&#96;opm&#96;** — Offensive BPM (same matched row as BPM).\\n- **&#96;dbpm&#96;** / **&#96;dpm&#96;** — Defensive BPM.\\n- **&#96;per&#96;** — Player efficiency rating; composite efficiency index.\\n- **&#96;ws40&#96;** — Win shares **per 40 minutes** (rate).\\n- **&#96;ws&#96;** — Win **shares** (season total at the matched row).\\n- **&#96;tspct&#96;** or **&#96;ts_pct&#96;** — True shooting **percentage** from SR (panel column &#96;ts_pct_sr&#96;).\\n\\n**&#96;perf_zscore_within_season&#96;** — If &#96;True&#96;, &#96;perf&#96; is replaced by its **z-score within each collegiate season** before LOO. Then &#96;poolq_loo&#96; is in **within-season SD units** (useful for comparing metrics or multi-metric plots later).\\n\\n### Bins on &#96;poolq_loo&#96; (&#96;ventiles&#96; + &#96;poolq_binning&#96;)\\n\\n- **&#96;ventiles&#96;** — Number of bins (default 20). The plot title says **ventiles** only when &#96;ventiles=20&#96; **and** &#96;poolq_binning=\\\"quantile\\\"&#96;: that is the usual “20 quantile groups,” i.e. ~5% of the sample per bin.\\n- **&#96;poolq_binning=\\\"quantile\\\"&#96;** — &#96;pd.qcut&#96; on &#96;poolq_loo&#96; (~equal **counts** per bin). Matches a 520-style **quantile** binning choice.\\n- **&#96;poolq_binning=\\\"equal_width\\\"&#96;** — &#96;pd.cut&#96; on the min–max range of &#96;poolq_loo&#96; (equal **width** on the x-axis). Matches 520 **equal_width**. Export filenames add &#96;_poolqeqwidth&#96; so you do not overwrite quantile runs.\\n\\n**Export filenames** — Slugs use the **active** perf key (first in the list for that run), plus optional &#96;_zwithinseason&#96;, and optional &#96;_poolqeqwidth&#96; for equal-width bins.\\n\\n### Unmatched schools (CELL 2 toggles)\\n\\n- **Draft** — &#96;print_unmatched_school_lists=True&#96; prints colleges from the NBA draft register that never mapped to your ESPN school linkage. Source QA file: &#96;datasets/mbb/draft_unmapped_colleges.csv&#96; (from your draft-match export).\\n- **Sports Reference** — Same toggle prints ESPN **teams** (with &#96;team_id&#96;, display name, &#96;school_slug&#96;) that still have player-season rows without a BPM merge hit after the last &#96;run_match&#96;. Source: &#96;datasets/mbb/bpm_panel_rows_unmatched.csv&#96;. Small religious colleges, HBCUs, NAIA, etc. often show up here when SR slugs or rosters do not line up—useful for planning manual aliases, conference-level imputation, or alternate pool-quality proxies.\"}}"
}
```
Status: **completed**


</details>
</tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="unknown" data-tool-name="mcp-notebook_mcp-user-notebook_mcp-notebook_edit_cell"><details>

_**Agent (default)**_

Here is what was added.

### Config: `ventile_eda_plot_style` (`sports_pipeline/config.py`)
- **`"poolq_line"`** (default) — Same as before: one axes, draft rate vs **mean** `poolq_loo`, optional LPM, three-line title with draftee flags.
- **`"bins_bars_520"`** — Matches legacy **530 Cell 11** / `_530_extract/cell_11.py`:
  - **Left:** `ax.bar` of mean draft rate vs **bin index 1 … K** (`steelblue`, `edgecolor="white"`, `linewidth=0.4`), labels like the old notebook.
  - **Right:** `poolq_loo` on the x-axis as **median within bin** (new column in the binned table), `marker="o"`, `ms=4`, `steelblue`.
  - **LPM:** If `show_quadratic_lpm_on_eda_plot` and enough rows, the dashed quadratic is drawn only on the **right** panel (same micro-level OLS as before).

Validated in `__post_init__` like the other enum-style fields.

### Binned CSV (`ventile_table`)
- New column **`poolq_median`** (with existing `poolq_mean`, `draft_rate`, `n`, `vent`).

### Filenames (`export_plot_slug` in `perf_metric.py`)
- With **`bins_bars_520`**, slug gets **`_ventilebars520`** so ventile PNG/CSV/provenance names stay distinct from the line plot.

### Notebook
- **CELL 2:** Comment + `ventile_eda_plot_style="poolq_line"` in `PipelineConfig` (flip to `"bins_bars_520"` when you want the bar layout).
- **Markdown (bins section):** Short description of the two styles and the slug suffix.

To use the 520-style figure, set in **CELL 2**:

```python
ventile_eda_plot_style="bins_bars_520",
```

---

_**User**_

1.  For this setting: ventile_eda_plot_style="bins_bars_520", I'd like to keep the metadata box beneath the plot, but I ONLY want the bar chart in the plot, not both the barchart and scatterplot side by side
2. for all of the plots with a mean Y-draft y-axis, I'd like the y axis to start at 0


---

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd "/Users/charleslevine/Library/CloudStorage/Dropbox/1-Documents/00- Dissertation/0-Next_Chapter/Code_and_Data/New SQL and PY Code/Cursor Workspace PDE" && python -c "
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from sports_pipeline.config import PipelineConfig
from sports_pipeline.panel_build import ventile_table, ventile_plot, ventile_provenance_lines

np.random.seed(0)
n = 800
df = pd.DataFrame({
    'poolq_loo': np.random.randn(n),
    'poolq_sq': np.random.randn(n)**2,
    'Y_draft': (np.random.rand(n) > 0.92).astype(int),
    'athlete_id': np.arange(n),
    'season': np.random.randint(2010, 2020, n),
    'team_id': np.random.randint(1, 50, n),
    'minutes': np.random.rand(n) * 500,
})
cfg = PipelineConfig(ventile_eda_plot_style='bins_bars_520', restrict_teams_by_draftees=False, ventile_plot_show_metadata=True)
bt = ventile_table(df, cfg)
prov = ventile_provenance_lines(df, cfg, bt)
ventile_plot(bt, cfg, n, use=df, provenance_lines=prov, show=False)
cfg2 = PipelineConfig(ventile_eda_plot_style='poolq_line', restrict_teams_by_draftees=False)
ventile_plot(bt, cfg2, n, use=df, provenance_lines=[], show=False)
print('ok')
"

```bash
import pandas as pd
```

```bash
cd "/Users/charleslevine/Library/CloudStorage/Dropbox/1-Documents/00- Dissertation/0-Next_Chapter/Code_and_Data/New SQL and PY Code/Cursor Workspace PDE" && python -c "
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from sports_pipeline.config import PipelineConfig
from sports_pipeline.panel_build import ventile_table, ventile_provenance_lines

np.random.seed(0)
n = 200
df = pd.DataFrame({
    'poolq_loo': np.random.randn(n),
    'poolq_sq': np.random.randn(n)**2,
    'Y_draft': (np.random.rand(n) > 0.9).astype(int),
    'athlete_id': np.arange(n),
    'season': 2015,
    'team_id': 1,
    'minutes': 100.0,
})
cfg = PipelineConfig(poolq_winsor_quantiles=(0.01, 0.99), restrict_teams_by_draftees=False)
bt = ventile_table(df, cfg)
lines = ventile_provenance_lines(df, cfg, bt)
assert any('winsorization' in L for L in lines)
print([L for L in lines if 'winsor' in L][0][:120], '...')
"

```

_**Agent (default)**_

Here is what was added.

### `ventile_provenance_lines` (metadata block + `.txt`)
When `cfg.poolq_winsor_quantiles` is not `None`, a paragraph is inserted **after** the ventile-binning line that states:
- the tuple, e.g. `(0.01, 0.99)`;
- that **after LOO** `poolq_loo` is **clipped** to the empirical lower/upper quantiles on **all** rows with defined `poolq_loo` at that step;
- that this is **before** `min_minutes` and the team-draftee filters on the ventile/LPM subsample;
- that **`poolq_sq`** is then `poolq_loo²`.

### `poolq_winsor_short_note(cfg)` helper
Returns e.g. `poolq_winsor_quantiles=(0.01, 0.99)` or `""` if winsor is off.

### `ventile_plot`
- **`bins_bars_520`** with **`ventile_plot_show_metadata=False`**: that short string is appended to the bottom **foot** line (with the other settings).
- **`poolq_line`** with **`ventile_plot_show_metadata=False`**: the same string is added as an extra **title** line (`… (clip poolq_loo after LOO)`).
- **`poolq_line`** with **metadata on**: winsor appears only in the caption (no duplicate line in the title).

Helper and provenance live in ```214:232:sports_pipeline/panel_build.py``` (helper) and the new block after the binning paragraph in `ventile_provenance_lines`.

This message is to Coda (the agent working with me on the Army Officer Data), Scout (The agent working with me on the college basketball data) and Vector (The ScholarGPT agent working with me on a paper to publish)
CODA & SCOUT and VECTOR:
GREAT News!!  Scout and I were able to duplicate the results we got in the Army Officer Data with the Collegiate Data.  Here are two plots, the first is an Army plot the second is the collegiate basketball plot.  I am going to send these results to one of my best friends at Yale University who used to be the Director of Sports Analytics and Stats at ESPN.
I am going to format the message like this:
Intro: We’re testing whether upward mobility depends on relative performance within nested talent pools, with stronger payoffs to pool quality in the middle of the hierarchy and diminishing returns at the elite tier where slots are scarce—first in a closed military promotion market, then in a transparent pro (or college) sports labor market as a public, replicable second setting. 
First Paragraph: A summary of what we found in terms of the Army data, specifically with regard to Pool minus mean senior rater and promotion/early promotion to Major, and what was the effect overall.
Second Paragraph:  A summary of the basketball work, what the data sources were, variables we put in, cleaning  decisions, and results we found.  Specifically this plot in min minutes =1, no team draftee restriction, variable is points per minute, 2011-2021 for both panels, etc.  

Third Paragraph, Can you help interpret the results, what do you think is happening? Do you know of anyone else doing similar draft analysis?

I am going to have Vector, our, Scholar GPT Agent draft the memo.  Our goal after consulting with Dr BrianMacDonald, my Buddy from Yale, will be to then try to recreate these same results with academic careers and tenure.
Here’s the Plan:
1.	VECTOR, CODA,and SCOUT:
a.	Read this message and understand the plan
2.	CODA and SCOUT:
a.	You will each prepare a document for each other and VECTOR that summarizes your work with me in light of the results we got so that everyone is on the same page.
b.	CODA: If you think I should just re-submit the document you gave me earlier to show SCOUT, that’s fine, just let me know.
c.	I will give CODA’s document to SCOUT and vice versa, and I will give both documents to VECTOR
d.	Now I would like for CODA and SCOUT To Each prepare a background document (having also read each other’s for context) to give to VECTOR.
3.	VECTOR:  You will then prepare the message to Brian.


---

_**Agent (default)**_

Here is a concise coordination note and the **VECTOR-ready draft** you can paste into ScholarGPT after CODA’s paragraph is dropped in.

---

### Who did what in *this* thread

- **Scout:** Read the plan. Wrote a handoff doc you can give CODA and VECTOR:  
  `current_documents/Scout_background_CBB_pool_quality_draft_rate.md`  
- **CODA:** Not in this environment—reuse or refresh your earlier Army write-up and add the exact **CIF / Q1–Q8 / pool-minus-mean SNR** wording and **N**s so VECTOR can write paragraph 1 without guessing.  
- **VECTOR:** Below is a **draft memo to Brian**; replace the **Army paragraph** with CODA’s text when you have it.

---

### Draft message to Brian MacDonald (for VECTOR to polish)

**Subject (optional):** Parallel evidence on “middle of the pool” payoffs — Army promotions and college-to-NBA draft incidence  

**Intro**  
We’re testing whether upward mobility depends on **relative performance within nested talent pools**, with **stronger payoffs to pool quality in the middle of the hierarchy** and **diminishing returns at the elite tier** where slots are scarce. We first estimated this in a **closed military promotion market** (officer careers), then built a **transparent, replicable** second setting using **public college basketball data** linked to **NBA draft outcomes**.

**Paragraph 1 — Army (CODA: insert precise wording here)**  
*[PLACEHOLDER: Summarize the Army analysis — e.g. binning of “pool minus mean senior rater” (or the exact construct from 520), competing-risks / **Final CIF** to Major or early promotion, Q1–Q8 pattern, sample, and the substantive takeaway in one sentence.]*

**Paragraph 2 — College basketball (Scout)**  
In college basketball we use **ESPN game-level box** data aggregated to **player–season–team** rows, **2011–2021**, with **`min_minutes = 1`** and **no** “team must have a draftee” restriction (`restrict_teams_by_draftees=False`). **Teammate pool quality** is **leave-one-out mean teammate points per minute (`ppm`)** within **(team, season)** — so the peer benchmark excludes the player’s own minutes and points. After LOO, we apply **`poolq_winsor_quantiles = (0.05, 0.95)`** on **`poolq_loo`**, then bin **`poolq_loo`** into **eight equal-width** ventiles. The outcome is **`Y_draft`**, an **ever-drafted** flag from the **NBA draft register** linked through our **draft–athlete lookup**. On a sample of roughly **83k player-seasons** and **45k athletes**, mean draft incidence is on the order of **~1–1.5%**; the **bar chart of mean draft rate by ventile** rises through the middle–upper bins and **drops in the top bin**, matching the **hump-shaped** pattern we see in the Army CIF-by-quantile figure.

**Paragraph 3 — Interpretation & related work**  
We read this as consistent with **competition within nested pools**: being around **better teammates** helps exposure and development up to a point, but the **marginal value of an “elite” teammate pool** may shrink if **scarce pro slots**, **sorting**, or **substitution of individual signal** dominates at the top. The pattern is also compatible with **selection** (elite pools concentrate players who were going to be drafted anyway, compressing relative variation in draft rates). Others work in adjacent space: **NBA draft prediction** and **combine/college stats** models (prediction rather than peer-pool LOO); **peer effects** in teams; and **tournament / rationed-promotion** ideas from organizational economics. We’re not claiming a single identified causal story yet—this is **descriptive parallelism** across institutions before we push toward **academic careers and tenure** as a third setting.

**Closing**  
We’d value your read on **whether the college design is persuasive as a public analogue**, and any pointers on **who’s done closest work** on **draft incidence vs peer quality** or **hierarchical mobility** in sports or academia.

---

### Interpretation note (short, for you and VECTOR)

The **top-bin dip** in draft rate can reflect **real diminishing returns**, **composition** (elite teams roster many future pros; **conditional** draft probability need not rise monotonically in raw bins), or **measurement** (PPM LOO is one proxy). Framing for Brian: **parallel qualitative shape** across two very different institutions, with **honest limits** on causality.

---

**Next step for you:** Send CODA this path so they can align their memo to the same subsection structure; merge CODA’s Army paragraph into the draft above; then VECTOR/ScholarGPT does a final tone pass for Brian. If you want **Scout** to add a sentence to the Scout doc once CODA’s doc exists, paste CODA’s summary here and we can cross-reference it in one pass.

---

_**Agent (default)**_

I don’t know of a **Cursor feature** that opens a private, automatic chat channel where **Scout**, **CODA**, and **PEER** talk to each other without you. In this repo, those names are **roles you use in docs** (`TENURE_PIPELINE_OVERVIEW.md`, handoff markdown, etc.), not a built-in “multi-agent dialogue” mode I can see wired up (no `AGENTS.md`, no `.cursor/rules` agent bridge in the workspace).

What people usually use instead:

1. **Shared file as the “wire”**  
   One markdown file (e.g. `current_documents/AGENT_INBOX.md` or your existing `WHO_GETS_WHAT` / handoffs) with **dated, signed blocks**: `## [2026-04-06] Scout → CODA: …`  
   You open the same file in each chat and ask each agent to **read the latest section and append a reply**. That *simulates* peer dialogue; you’re still the router.

2. **Project rules**  
   `.cursor/rules/*.mdc` can say things like “when working on 520, read `army_documents/…`; when on 530, read `Scout_background…`” so different **sessions** stay aligned. Still not agent-to-agent DMs.

3. **Single Composer / Agent thread**  
   You can **@** multiple files from CODA + Scout + PEER in **one** conversation and ask for a merged answer—that’s one agent session with shared context, not two agents talking alone.

4. **External “Vector”**  
   Browser tools are separate; you bridge them by **upload/paste**, as you did.

If you saw something advertised as **peer agents** or **multi-agent chat**, it may be a **very new Cursor Labs / changelog** item—I don’t have a definitive name for it. Worth checking **Cursor Docs → Changelog** or **Settings** for anything like “multi-agent” or “team.”

**Practical recommendation:** treat **`Vector_Browser_Package_FULL_TEXT.md`**-style bundles plus an optional **`AGENT_INBOX.md`** as your “dialogue layer”; it’s explicit, versionable, and works in any editor.
